137185.explanation: Wij zijn tegen een volledig verbod op fatbikes in de binnenstad. Extra verboden lossen weinig op. Het gaat om gedrag en naleving van regels. In echte wandelgebieden zijn beperkingen logisch. Verder kiezen wij voor beter toezicht en handhaving in plaats van betutteling en nieuwe regels.

137186.explanation: Wij zijn voor camera’s bij afvalinzamelpunten om dumpgedrag tegen te gaan. De inzet moet gericht en functioneel zijn: alleen op het inzamelpunt zelf. Privacy moet goed worden beschermd en er mag niet meer worden vastgelegd dan nodig. Zo houden we de omgeving schoon zonder onnodige inbreuk.

137187.explanation: Wij zijn tegen een vast percentage van 40% sociale huur. Woningbouw moet passen bij de behoefte van een wijk of dorp. Harde quota kunnen bouw vertragen. Wij willen vooral betaalbare woningen en een logische mix per gebied, zodat er echt gebouwd wordt waar behoefte aan is.

137193.explanation: Wij zijn voor het slopen van het voormalige Centraal Beheergebouw. Het pand is iconisch, maar voegt in de huidige staat te weinig toe. Deze plek is hard nodig voor nieuwe woningen. Niet behouden om het behouden, maar kiezen voor wat Apeldoorn vooruithelpt.

137196.explanation: Wij vinden dat maatschappelijk zinvol werk een meerwaarde kan zijn voor mensen met een bijstandsuitkering. Het mag nooit een straf zijn. Het draait om motiveren en begeleiden om mee te doen. Wie door gezondheid echt niet kan, mag geen extra druk ervaren. Ondersteunen waar het kan, rust waar het moet.

137202.explanation: Parkeren in het centrum moet betaalbaar blijven. Veel inwoners, vooral uit dorpen en wijken, zijn afhankelijk van de auto. Te hoge tarieven schrikken bezoekers af en raken ondernemers. Betaalbaar parkeren houdt het centrum bereikbaar en ondersteunt een sterke lokale economie.

137206.explanation: Wij zijn tegen het uitbreiden van autovrije straten in de binnenstad. De binnenstad moet goed bereikbaar blijven voor jou, ondernemers en bezoekers. Veel inwoners, vooral uit dorpen en buitengebieden, zijn afhankelijk van de auto. Meer autovrije straten maken de stad minder toegankelijk.

137207.explanation: Wij vinden dat verkeersmaatregelen logisch moeten zijn. 30 km/u past in woonstraten, bij scholen en zorginstellingen waar veiligheid voorop staat, maar niet op alle wegen. Hoofdwegen moeten hun doorstromende functie houden. Een algemene 30 km-regel zorgt voor omrijden. BBB kiest voor maatwerk.

137208.explanation: Wij willen dat Apeldoorn goed bereikbaar blijft en dat nieuwe infrastructuur logisch wordt ingericht. Een spoortunnel vraagt om een passende toeleidende weg. Door de Laan van Spitsbergen bij de tunnel 2×2 rijstroken te geven blijft verkeer doorrijden en verbeteren de aanrijtijden van hulpdiensten.

137212.explanation: BBB is voor het verhogen van de mantelzorgvergoeding van €50 naar €150 per jaar. Mantelzorg is vaak meer dan een baan en verdient waardering en steun. Wie dag in, dag uit klaarstaat voor een naaste mag er niet alleen voor staan en moet hulp kunnen krijgen als het te zwaar wordt.

137215.explanation: Wij zijn tegen het stoppen van de jacht op gemeentegronden. Goed faunabeheer is nodig om de natuur in balans te houden en kwetsbare dieren te beschermen. Het gaat niet om plezierjacht, maar om verantwoord ingrijpen wanneer populaties uit de hand lopen en schade of dierenleed dreigt.

137217.explanation: Wij zijn voor het helpen van ondernemers om hun gebouwen toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een beperking. Dat moet realistisch en betaalbaar blijven, met oog voor de rol van de ondernemer. Ondersteuning is logisch waar het effect heeft en mensen echt helpt om mee te doen.

233683.explanation: Wij willen de ozb niet verhogen, maar juist verlagen. Eerst moet de gemeente haar begroting op orde brengen en stoppen met prestigeprojecten. Lastenverlichting moet echt zijn en mag niet via andere heffingen alsnog bij inwoners worden teruggehaald.

233687.explanation: Terrassen moeten langer open kunnen blijven. Ondernemers verdienen ruimte om te ondernemen en wij vertrouwen op eigen verantwoordelijkheid. Wel moet het passen bij het gebied en goed worden afgestemd, zodat overlast wordt voorkomen. Zo blijft het gezellig en leefbaar voor iedereen.

233689.explanation: Apeldoorn moet afzien van grootschalige woningbouw aan de andere kant van de A1 en A50. Deze open gebieden zijn waardevol voor landbouw, natuur en leefbaarheid en bovendien laag en nat. Eerst benutten wat er al is: leegstand, ombouw en slimme verdichting bij bestaande dorpen.

233691.explanation: Apeldoorn moet stoppen met het formeel zijn van een regenbooggemeente. Jij moet jezelf kunnen zijn zonder labels. Veiligheid, respect en gelijke behandeling horen bij normaal gemeentelijk beleid voor iedereen, ook voor lhbtiqa+-inwoners. Dat regel je breed en structureel, niet via een apart label.

137153.explanation: Het gemeentelijk referendum is een belangrijk instrument voor inwoners om hun mening te kunnen geven over belangrijke beslissingen in de gemeente. De drempel daarvoor moet omlaag. Zeker in een tijd met afkalvend draagvlak voor de gemeente.

137158.explanation: Hondenbelasting is een doelbelasting en totale afschaffing betekent dat alle inwoners (ook niet hondenbezitters) gaan meebetalen aan kosten voor hondenuitlaatplaatsen etc. Dat gaat ons te ver. Daarbij komt dat Apeldoorn de hondenbelasting afgelopen jaren al heeft verlaagd.

137164.explanation: BVA kiest voor steun aan inwoners voor maatschappelijke initiatieven, niet voor onrendabele ondernemingen.

137165.explanation: Apeldoorners met binding verdienen voorrang op nieuwkomers. Zodra wachtlijsten voor sociale huurwoningen/eerstelijns zorg zijn weggewerkt kan er gewerkt worden aan spreiding.

137166.explanation: BVA kiest voor een bosbouwbeleid met gezond verstand, dus niet op basis van ideologische automatismen.

137169.explanation: BVA is duidelijk tegen de willekeur van een milieuzone, die kleine zelfstandigen en Apeldoorners met te weinig geld voor elektrisch rijden uitsluit.

137171.explanation: Toeristen zijn belangrijk voor ons en zij weten Apeldoorn goed te vinden. Het verlagen van (toeristen)belasting en verbetering van bewegwijzering helpt beter dan geld stoppen in campagnes van dure bureaus.

137172.explanation: BVA kiest onverkort voor privacy. Het verzamelen van data over mensen buiten hen om, hoort daar niet bij. Ook vanwege bijkomende straling en andere ruimtelijke effecten. Geen "witte kastjes" 2.0 in Apeldoorn.

137173.explanation: Het Zwitsalterrein is sinds jaar en dag bekend als hèt evenementen terrein vlakbij het centrum. Zolang de hinder beperkt blijft, dient dit zo te blijven.

137177.explanation: BVA vindt muzikale vorming belangrijk. Dat vraagt om vertrouwen in en steun voor muziekverenigingen, niet om inkomenssteun waardoor middengroepen buitengesloten worden.

137178.explanation: BVA geeft de breedtesport de prioriteit. Topsport kan een voorbeeldfunctie vervullen. In zijn geheel stoppen met geld voor topsport gaat veel te ver.

137181.explanation: BVA kiest voor boa-handhavers die veilig hun functie kunnen uitoefenen, door adequate steun van de politie. Het geven van taken aan boa's waarvoor zij niet zijn en het verstrekken van geweldsmiddelen kan leiden tot ongewenste escalatie.

137183.explanation: BVA kiest voor bescherming van bewoners. Nu blijkt dat hoge hekken niet helpen tegen het gevaar van wolven, past daar een actief wilbeheer bij.

137185.explanation: Fatbikes horen niet thuis in de binnenstad tussen het winkelend publiek. Zonder valhelmplicht is het onveilig en trekt het een onevenredige wissel op onze handhavers en inwoners.

137186.explanation: Camera's zijn geen doekje voor het bloeden. Het huidige afvalbeleid maakt het inwoners moeilijk afval kwijt te raken en leidt tot overlast en vervuiling van de straat. Dat wordt niet minder door camera’s die inbreken op privacy.

137187.explanation: BVA kiest voor woningcorporaties die doen waarvoor zij bedoeld zijn, het voorzien in sociale woningen. Het stellen van onrealistische eisen helpt niet en schrikt bouwers af. Wij geloven in zijn goede samenwerking en afspraken met woningcorporaties.

137193.explanation: BVA is vóór iconische nieuwbouw met woningen, door sloop van deze bouwval. Nog meer geld besteden aan onmogelijke plannen op deze stationslocatie vindt BVA onverantwoord.

137196.explanation: Inwoners zonder werk ontvangen een uitkering. Zo lang zij niet arbeidsongeschikt zijn, kunnen zij maatschappelijk zinvol werk doen of geholpen worden met opleiding of het opdoen van ervaring om weer aan de slag te kunnen.

137202.explanation: BVA kiest voor een beter bereikbare binnenstad. Daar hoort goedkooper parkeren (blauwe kaart voor kort parkeren) bij.

137206.explanation: De bereikbaarheid van de binnenstad is essentieel voor ondernemers en inwoners. Autootje pesten helpt dan niet. Verkeersbeleid moet gebaseerd zijn op meten, evalueren en bijsturen, niet op ideologie.

137207.explanation: Beperkingen aan eigen vervoer, zoals een zero-emissiezone of 30 km/u op de centrumring, ondermijnen het vrije verkeer. Verkeersbeleid moet gebaseerd zijn op meten, evalueren en bijsturen, niet op ideologie.

137208.explanation: Een vlotte doorstroming van het verkeer dient de veiligheid. BVA is voor een nieuw verkeersrouteplan voor Apeldoorn om verkeersoverlast te vermijden en veiligheid te dienen. Met gezond verstand. Kleinschalig buurtvervoer past daar bij.

137212.explanation: Mantelzorgers vervullen een belangrijke maatschappelijke taak. Na jarenlang korten op de vergoeding is verhoging van de vergoeding menselijk en wenselijk. Het gaat om een relatief gering bedrag.

137215.explanation: Actief wildbeheer kan het nodig maken dat er gejaagd wordt.

137217.explanation: Het voldoen aan eisen voor toegankelijkheid dient niet afhankelijk te worden van overheidsgeld. Wel dienen de eisen te worden verscherpt.

233683.explanation: De gemeente heeft een begroting van meer dan 800 miljoen. BVA streeft naar een kleinere meer slagvaardige gemeente, dus met lagere lasten.

233687.explanation: De openingstijden kunnen in overleg meer in overeenstemming gebracht worden met de wensen van horecaondernemers en de behoefte van consumenten.

233689.explanation: BVA wil bouwen op de stationslocatie en kiest voor inbreiding en kleinschalige woningbouwprojecten (voor ouderen). BVA is tegen de groei-ambitie om mensen van buiten te trekken, terwijl voor Apeldoorners de basis niet op orde is.

233691.explanation: Regenbooggemeente is geen doel op zich. De gemeente is van iedereen en dient zich te onthouden van politieke uittingen. Discriminatie dient te worden tegengegaan.

statements.theme: Paid parking in the city centre
statements.title: Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
statements.pro: Some parties think cheaper parking makes it more attractive to come to the city center by car. It is also said that many residents depend on cars. Furthermore, parties believe cheaper parking is good for downtown businesses.
statements.con: Other parties argue that cheaper parking leads visitors to come less often on foot, by bike, or by public transport. There is also a party that believes parking rates in Apeldoorn are not high. Finally, one party argues that cheaper parking will not, or will hardly, lead to more visitors to the city center.

statements.title: The former Centraal Beheer building on Prins Willem-Alexanderlaan should be demolished.
statements.moreInfo: the former Centraal Beheer building is part of an office complex from 1972 designed by Herman Hertzberger and is now owned by the municipality. The municipality is exploring new functions for the building. The associated high office towers will be demolished. The Cultural Heritage Agency of the Netherlands wants the building to become a national monument.
statements.pro: Some parties think it is too difficult to convert the former Centraal Beheer building into a residential building that meets current standards. It is also said that preserving the building entails large financial risks. Furthermore, parties believe the building is outdated.
statements.con: Other parties believe that the internationally known building attracts people to come to Apeldoorn. There is also a party that thinks the building is valuable heritage connected to Apeldoorn's history. Finally, parties argue that the housing shortage can be effectively addressed in this building.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.
statements.pro: Some parties believe the dog tax discourages dog ownership, whereas dogs actually help combat loneliness. It is also said that it is unfair for dog owners to contribute to facilities used by all residents. Furthermore, one party believes that collecting a dog tax costs more than it yields.
statements.con: Other parties think dog owners should contribute to the costs of dog exercise fields. There are also parties that believe income from dog tax is needed for liveability and the performance of core tasks in Apeldoorn. Finally, one party argues that it is unfair if all residents have to pay for facilities used only by dog owners.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: Homeowners can install solar panels on their roof if it is suitable. If that is not possible, people can join others to buy or rent land for one or more wind turbines or solar parks so they can still invest in sustainable energy. Sustainable energy is generated from sources that never run out, such as sunlight and wind. Collective generation of sustainable energy often takes place in the form of energy cooperatives.
statements.pro: Some parties think municipal support for generating sustainable energy accelerates the transition to renewable energy sources. It is also said that low-income households will have easier access to sustainable energy this way. Furthermore, one party believes support for sustainable energy strengthens engagement and public support for sustainable solutions.
statements.con: Other parties believe there are already sufficient national schemes for generating sustainable energy. There are also parties that think residents should pay the costs themselves of generating sustainable energy. Finally, one party argues that the municipality should not support unprofitable enterprises.

statements.title: The municipality should stop spending money on top-level sporting events.
statements.pro: Some parties believe top-level sporting events cost a lot of money. It is also said that these kinds of events bring nothing to the municipality. Furthermore, one party believes top sports events only reach a limited group of residents.
statements.con: Other parties believe top sports events inspire people to start exercising themselves. There are also parties that think these kinds of events can generate extra income for entrepreneurs in Apeldoorn. Finally, one party argues that top sports events are an enjoyable experience for residents and tourists.

statements.title: The speed limit on all roads within the central ring should be 30 km/h.
statements.pro: Some parties believe a 30 km/h speed limit is safer for cyclists and pedestrians. It is also said that this creates more space for greenery, improving air quality. Furthermore, parties believe a lower speed limit makes the city center more liveable.
statements.con: Other parties argue that a lower maximum speed within the city ring causes unnecessary traffic movements because people will take detours. There are also parties that think a 30 km/h limit is only needed on residential streets and near schools and care institutions. Finally, one party argues that this measure does not outweigh the costs.

statements.title: The municipality should allow new coffeeshops in Apeldoorn.
statements.pro: Some parties believe there is a need for an additional coffeeshop in Apeldoorn. Furthermore, parties think extra coffeeshops need not be a problem as long as they are not located near schools.
statements.con: Other parties believe that additional coffeeshops add nothing. Finally, parties argue that coffeeshops often cause nuisance in the surrounding area.

statements.title: Apeldoorn should increase the compensation for informal caregivers from €50 to €150 per year.
statements.pro: Some parties think increasing the carer’s allowance is an appropriate token of appreciation for a demanding task. It is also said that this measure helps prevent carers from becoming overburdened. Furthermore, parties believe caregiving is more than a job.
statements.con: Other parties believe practical support is more effective than a symbolic allowance. There is also a party that thinks it is a token of appreciation rather than a payment. Finally, parties argue that caregivers are better helped with respite care than with an allowance.

statements.title: More car-free streets should be created in Apeldoorn's city centre.
statements.pro: Some parties think car-free streets make the city center much more pleasant. It is also said that less car traffic provides space for more greenery, which has a positive effect on people's health. Furthermore, parties believe the city center is for pedestrians and cyclists.
statements.con: Other parties consider it very important for entrepreneurs, residents and visitors that the city center remains easily accessible by car. There is also a party that believes more car-free streets are unnecessary because the shopping area is getting smaller. Finally, one party thinks more car-free streets are only possible if there is sufficient free or very cheap public transport.

statements.title: If the municipality cuts down a tree, two new ones must be planted.
statements.moreInfo: The obligation to plant two new trees would only apply if the municipality fells a tree that stands on municipal land; it would not apply to trees outside municipal land.
statements.pro: Some parties believe trees are important for the climate and liveability. It is also said that the municipality should set a good example for its residents. Furthermore, one party believes this keeps the municipality's green spaces at a good level.
statements.con: Other parties find a fixed rule of two new trees for each felled tree undesirable. There is also a party that believes the municipality should not introduce extra rules. Finally, one party argues that this depends on the location.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute holders of refugee status across all neighborhoods and villages in the municipality.
statements.pro: Some parties believe dispersal promotes the integration of refugees with residence permits. Furthermore, parties believe this preserves liveability in all neighborhoods and villages.
statements.con: Other parties believe that status holders are ordinary residents who do not need special treatment. Finally, one party argues that when spreading them around, there is not always good consideration of what a neighborhood can handle.

statements.title: The Laan van Spitsbergen should be widened to two lanes in each direction at the location of the planned rail tunnel.
statements.moreInfo: Laan van Spitsbergen is part of Apeldoorn’s ring road. The municipality plans to replace the level railway crossing there with a tunnel with 2×1 lanes, but there is debate about whether the tunnel should immediately be widened to 2×2 lanes.
statements.pro: Some parties believe a two-by-two lane layout is better for traffic safety. There are also parties who think traffic will flow better that way. Furthermore, one party believes the municipality should tackle this bottleneck properly in one go.
statements.con: Other parties find two-by-two lanes too expensive. It is also said that this measure shifts the traffic jam to the ring road. Finally, parties argue that widening the tunnel is not necessary at all.

statements.title: The municipality should stop hunting wild animals on municipal land.
statements.moreInfo: In the Netherlands hunting is allowed for some wild animals. Hunting large game such as roe deer, red deer and wild boar is permitted only with provincial approval. As a landowner the municipality of Apeldoorn can choose to prohibit hunting on municipal land.
statements.pro: Some parties believe all wild animals deserve respect. It is also said that all animals contribute to biodiversity. Furthermore, parties believe the municipality must safeguard animals' right to life.
statements.con: Other parties argue that hunting is sometimes necessary to prevent nuisance or damage. There are also parties that believe hunting can sometimes increase biodiversity. Finally, parties argue that hunting sometimes helps prevent the spread of diseases.

statements.title: The municipality should install cameras at waste collection points.
statements.pro: Some parties believe cameras help prevent additional dumping or illegal disposal of waste. It is also said the measure contributes to a cleaner living environment. Furthermore, parties believe cameras will ultimately lead to lower waste collection costs.
statements.con: Other parties argue that cameras can harm residents' privacy. There are also parties that think measures like greening and waste coaches are more effective at preventing illegally placed waste. Finally, one party argues that cameras do not solve the problem that residents sometimes have difficulty disposing of their waste.

statements.title: Anyone receiving a social assistance benefit should, in return, perform socially useful work.
statements.pro: Some parties believe socially useful work is good for people receiving social assistance benefits. It is also said that doing socially useful work is a way to contribute to society. Furthermore, parties believe it is important that everyone participates in society.
statements.con: Other parties believe that socially useful work must not be made compulsory. There is also a party that thinks residents should not have their welfare benefit reduced if they refuse this work. Finally, several parties argue that not everyone receiving welfare is able to do socially useful work.

statements.title: The number of signatures required to start a referendum should be reduced. Currently more than 6,000 signatures are needed.
statements.moreInfo: In a referendum the government puts one question to voters, who can vote for or against. In a municipal referendum all residents of the municipality aged 18 or older may vote. Both the municipal council and ordinary citizens can request a referendum; if citizens want a referendum they must collect signatures. Municipal rules state the minimum number required; in Apeldoorn this is more than 6,000 (5% of about 130,000 eligible voters). In the Netherlands a referendum result is not binding, meaning the government does not have to follow it.
statements.pro: Some parties believe a lower number of signatures increases citizen involvement. It is also said that the current number of signatures is very high compared to other municipalities of similar size. Furthermore, parties believe every citizen should have the opportunity to exert influence.
statements.con: Other parties believe the current number of signatures works well. There are also parties that think referenda should only be a supplement to decision-making in the municipal council. Finally, parties argue that residents can already easily bring an issue important to them to the attention of politicians.

statements.theme: Pepper spray for municipal enforcement officers (BOAs)
statements.title: Apeldoorn should equip municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who enforce public safety with pepper spray.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for enforcement of minor offences, such as parking without paying, cycling on the sidewalk, or littering in nature. People who do this receive a fine. The municipality’s enforcers are BOAs (special enforcement officers). Police officers are general investigative officers with different tasks and powers. If the municipality wants to equip BOAs with additional tools such as pepper spray, it must demonstrate the need and apply to the minister, who is advised by the police and Public Prosecution Service. BOAs in Apeldoorn already have handcuffs.
statements.pro: Some parties believe enforcement officers (BOAs) should be better equipped against increasing violence. Furthermore, parties believe pepper spray increases BOAs' safety.
statements.con: Other parties believe that the use of pepper spray can provoke counter-violence. Finally, one party states that only the police should be allowed to use means such as pepper spray.

statements.title: Apeldoorn should completely ban riding fatbikes in the city centre.
statements.pro: Some parties think a complete ban on fatbikes is good for safety in the city center. Furthermore, parties believe people often ride fatbikes recklessly and cause nuisance.
statements.con: Another party believes the municipality should enforce behavior, not the type of vehicle. Finally, one party states that the municipality cannot prohibit fatbikes separately from other means of transport.

statements.title: Apeldoorn should stop collecting additional data in public spaces.
statements.moreInfo: Apeldoorn collects data through sensors in the public space that measure, among other things, temperature, fine particulate matter, noise and traffic flows. With the collected data the municipality wants insight into the effects of climate change, traffic and tourism on the city.
statements.pro: Some parties believe the municipality must not collect data about people without their knowledge. It is also said the equipment used may emit radiation. Furthermore, one party believes data collection makes Apeldoorn dependent on large tech companies and other commercial parties.
statements.con: Other parties believe that collecting certain data can increase safety. There are also parties that think the data helps to improve traffic flow. Finally, parties argue that the municipality can use the data to carry out its tasks more efficiently.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the province to shoot wolves in Apeldoorn.
statements.moreInfo: The wolf is a protected species. If a wolf shows abnormal behaviour or causes problems, the municipality cannot decide to shoot it. That authority lies with the province, which can grant permission only in exceptional cases.
statements.pro: Some parties believe wolves do not belong in the city and villages. It is also said the municipality should pursue flexible management rules, including by lobbying the national government and the European Union. Furthermore, parties believe the mayor should be given more powers in wolf-related emergencies.
statements.con: Other parties believe wolves are part of nature. There is also a party that thinks livestock can be protected from wolves with fences and dogs. Finally, one party argues that the municipality can better provide education about living together with wildlife.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.
statements.pro: Some parties believe making buildings more accessible strengthens the participation of people with disabilities in society. Furthermore, one party believes the municipality should help small businesses that cannot afford this themselves (but not large companies).
statements.con: Other parties believe that making buildings more accessible is not a task for the municipality. There are also parties that think entrepreneurs should take care of that themselves.

statements.title: The Zwitsalterrein along the Apeldoorn Canal should remain an event site.
statements.moreInfo: The Zwitsal site is a former factory location along the Apeldoorns Canal, now used by creative entrepreneurs and for events. The municipality wants 550 new homes on part of the site. There is debate about the future of the site as an events location once people live there.
statements.pro: There are parties that consider events in this area important for Apeldoorn's attractiveness and vibrancy.
statements.con: Other parties believe that events can cause too much nuisance for local residents.

statements.title: Apeldoorn should withdraw the plan to introduce an environmental zone in the city centre in 2030.
statements.moreInfo: Some cities have an environmental zone, usually in the inner city, which bans vehicles that emit many pollutants from entering. This often applies to trucks, vans and diesel cars. Apeldoorn has decided to introduce a zero-emission zone on 1 January 2030, meaning only delivery vans and trucks without harmful emissions may drive there. This decision follows the national Climate Agreement, in which the forty largest municipalities agreed to introduce such zones.
statements.pro: One party finds implementing the plan in 2030 unrealistic. It is also said that it would be better to first tackle the hundred largest polluting companies. Furthermore, one party believes local businesses in the city center suffer because fewer customers come.
statements.con: Other parties believe that an environmental zone contributes to cleaner air in the city center. There are also parties that think Apeldoorn is legally obliged to establish an environmental zone. Finally, parties argue that some businesses have already spent money to comply with the new requirements later.

statements.title: Property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) may be raised to maintain municipal services.
statements.pro: Some parties believe important facilities should remain accessible to residents. It is also said the municipality must continue investing in people, animals, and nature. Furthermore, parties believe the residents of Apeldoorn want to preserve the quality of municipal services.
statements.con: Other parties think the municipality should have fewer ambitions instead of raising the property tax (ozb). Some parties believe Apeldoorn should try to save money, for example by working more efficiently. Finally, one party argues the municipality should stop pursuing prestige projects.

statements.title: Apeldoorn should stop running campaigns to attract more tourists.
statements.pro: Some parties believe tourism and recreation often come at the expense of nature. It is also said the city is for the residents, not for tourists. Furthermore, one party believes campaigns to attract more tourists are not effective.
statements.con: Other parties believe more tourists are good for local businesses because they spend more money. Some parties also think tourism creates jobs. Finally, one party argues the municipality can use the income from the tourist tax.

statements.title: At least 40% of all new homes to be built in Apeldoorn should be social rental housing.
statements.pro: Some parties believe there is strong demand for social rental housing in Apeldoorn. It is also said everyone has a right to affordable housing. Furthermore, parties believe the municipality must act quickly on the long waiting lists for social rental housing.
statements.con: Other parties believe Apeldoorn should build homes at all price levels. Some parties think the proceeds from more expensive homes are needed to make social housing possible. Finally, one party argues that a fixed percentage can slow down the construction of homes.

statements.title: Apeldoorn should stop being a rainbow municipality.
statements.pro: Some parties believe the label "rainbow municipality" is unnecessary to show that everyone is equal. It is also said the municipality should not place target groups in a separate position. Furthermore, one party believes the municipality should refrain from political expressions, such as raising a rainbow flag.
statements.con: Other parties believe that, as a "rainbow municipality," Apeldoorn contributes to the safety and equal opportunities of residents who face discrimination and exclusion. Some parties also think the municipality must ensure equal rights and acceptance. Finally, parties argue that attention to LGBTIQA+ projects strengthens social cohesion.

statements.theme: No housing on the other side of the A1 or A50
statements.title: Apeldoorn should refrain from building new housing on the other side of the A1 or the A50.
statements.moreInfo: Apeldoorn plans to build homes on municipal land in Beekbergsebroek (south of the A1).
statements.pro: Some parties believe the municipal lands in Beekbergsebroek are valuable for agriculture, nature, and recreation. It is also said the municipality can better seek creative solutions for housing within the existing city. Furthermore, one party believes the area is low-lying and too wet for housing.
statements.con: Other parties believe there is too little space for housing in the existing city. Some parties think it is the municipality's task to ensure there are enough homes. Finally, one party argues that building in Beekbergsebroek prevents green spaces in existing neighborhoods from being sacrificed.

statements.title: Outdoor terraces in Apeldoorn should be allowed to stay open longer.
statements.moreInfo: The terraces in Apeldoorn’s nightlife area may stay open until 1:00 AM, including the Marktplein, Marktstraat and the KPN courtyard. Outside the nightlife area the closing time from March through October is midnight.
statements.pro: Some parties believe entrepreneurs should have the freedom to decide their own closing times. It is also said terraces that stay open longer make the city centre attractive and lively. Furthermore, one party believes more young people will come to Apeldoorn if terraces stay open later.
statements.con: Other parties think the current closing times for terraces are fine. Some parties believe the municipality should give more consideration to inner-city residents. Finally, one party argues the municipality should protect Sunday rest.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.
statements.pro: Some parties believe every child should be able to develop culturally. It is also said money must not be a barrier to taking music lessons. Furthermore, one party believes culture gives children chances to develop and to relax.
statements.con: Other parties think many parents can pay for music lessons themselves. Some parties believe paying for music lessons is not a core task of the municipality. Finally, parties argue that giving money to music associations to mentor children is more effective.
